title: Knowledge Transfer Program
when_to_use: Use Knowledge Transfer Program to develop professional-grade knowledge transfer program capabilities at Mastery level. This recipe is part of the Red Team Leadership & Practice pillar and integrates with linked missions OM-M12, OM-M13, OM-M14, OM-M15.

steps.title: Program Architecture
steps.instruction: Design comprehensive training program (8+ pages): tiers (beginner, intermediate, advanced, mastery), progression pathways, assessment criteria, graduation requirements. Example: "Advanced tier requires 6 months, completion of 5 projects, peer review approval, and mastery project assessment." Include: prerequisites, estimated time investment, success metrics, community roles.
steps.tip: Program architecture determines who graduates and what level they achieve. Be rigorous; reputation depends on graduate quality.

steps.title: Learning Paths for Diverse Profiles
steps.instruction: Design 3 distinct learning paths: (1) Recent graduate track (weak hacking background, strong fundamentals needed), (2) IT professional transition (strong infrastructure background, needs red team mindset), (3) SOC analyst track (detection-focused, needs offensive perspective). Each path: 12-month progression, monthly milestones, learning objectives, project work. Paths converge at mastery.
steps.tip: Diversity of backgrounds requires tailored paths. Learning customization increases success rate.
steps.expected_output: 3 detailed learning paths with monthly milestones

steps.title: Complete Training Module Creation
steps.instruction: Create one complete training module from your expertise area: slides (50+ slides, narrative flow), lab guide (hands-on exercises with detailed steps), assessment (knowledge check quiz, practical challenge, project). Module should teach a capstone skill: "Kernel Exploitation for EDR Bypass" or "Cloud-Native Attack Methodology". Include: learning objectives, prerequisite knowledge, expected time, success criteria.
steps.tip: Creating training reveals gaps in your knowledge. Teaching forces clarity.
steps.expected_output: Complete training module: slides, lab guide, assessment

steps.title: Community Contribution Plan
steps.instruction: Annual plan for knowledge sharing: blog posts (12+ per year, e.g., "EDR Evasion Techniques", "Cloud Exploitation Patterns"), speaking engagements (2-3 conferences), open-source contributions (release tools, share code), mentorship (3-5 mentees). Plan: topics, timeline, venues, community platforms. This positions you as thought leader.
steps.tip: Community contribution builds reputation and attracts quality people to your program.
steps.expected_output: Annual contribution plan with quarterly deliverables

steps.title: Program Measurement Framework
steps.instruction: Design success metrics: graduate employment rate (90%+ in red team roles?), graduate satisfaction (survey feedback), graduate advancement (how many move to leadership?), employer feedback (are graduates performing well?), retention (how many stay in security field?). Measurement enables continuous improvement.
steps.tip: Metrics drive improvements. Measure what matters: graduate success, not just completion.
steps.expected_output: Measurement framework with KPIs and survey instruments

steps.title: Personal Commitment Statement
steps.instruction: Write your covenant with the community (1 page): what you commit to (knowledge sharing, mentorship, raising next generation), how you'll live it (concrete actions), why you believe it (your philosophy). This statement guides program decisions and inspires students. Share it publicly to hold yourself accountable.
steps.tip: Commitment statement should be authentic and visceral. It's your "why".
